translations.descricao: Acne vulgaris is a chronic inflammatory condition of the pilosebaceous unit that commonly affects adults, adolescents, and pre-adolescents aged 9 years or older. It is one of the most common skin conditions in children and adolescents, with presentation, differential diagnosis, and associations with systemic pathologies varying by age. Management involves topical, systemic, and dermocosmetic approaches, with a negative impact on quality of life, including anxiety and depression. Associated conditions such as polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) may contribute to acne in women.
translations.sus.indicacao: In the SUS, acne management is recommended for patients with moderate to severe lesions, prioritizing accessible treatments and Latin American dermatological protocols.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adolescents and adults with symptomatic acne vulgaris, including those with comorbidities such as PCOS.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The guidelines of the American Academy of Dermatology (AAD) recommend management based on severity, with topical therapies for mild, combination for moderate, and systemic for severe. Endocrine Society for acne associated with PCOS.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: High prevalence in adolescents and young adults, commonly affecting pediatric and adult populations. Global prevalence estimated at up to 80-90% during adolescence.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Incidence increases during puberty, with peaks at 12-24 years.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Negligible mortality, but significant morbidity due to scarring and psychological impact.
